subheadline: Create stunning apps & websites by chatting with AI.
value_proposition: An AI-powered app and website builder that handles coding, infrastructure, and deployment in one platform so non-engineers can ship production-ready products fast.
target_audience: Product managers, entrepreneurs, marketers, agencies, and students who want to build apps or websites without deep engineering expertise

pricing.price_anchoring: Leads with 'start building for free' at the bottom CTA; Pricing link in nav implies tiered plans exist but no price anchoring is shown on the page

features: AI chat-based app and website builder
Frontier coding agent integrations from multiple AI labs
Built-in hosting with analytics and custom domains
Design system import (Figma, GitHub, component libraries)
differentiators: 'Always the best, without switching tools' — single interface for multiple frontier AI agents
'98% less errors' through automatic testing, refactoring, and iteration
'Projects 1,000 times larger than before' with improved context management
Your company's design system built in — Porsche, Washington Post, Material UI etc.
trust_signals: Claims '#1 professional vibe coding tool trusted by' (though specific customer names are cut off in the text)
Named enterprise design system partners: Porsche, Washington Post

design_patterns.layout: Full-width hero with centered CTA, followed by feature sections with supporting metrics, a role-based value prop grid, and a closing CTA — classic SaaS top-down narrative flow
design_patterns.color_scheme: Likely dark background with high-contrast accent (typical for AI/dev tools); cannot confirm from text alone
design_patterns.typography: Bold display headline, clean sans-serif body — modern and confident
design_patterns.visual_style: Bold and aspirational with technical credibility signals
notable_patterns: Design system logo carousel / showcase section
Role-based segmentation cards (PM, Entrepreneur, Marketer, Agency, Student)
Stat callouts ('98% less errors', '1,000 times larger')
Import integrations (Figma, GitHub) as hero trust signals

emotional_tone: Aspirational and confident — 'What will you build today?' invites possibility; '#1 tool' and bold metrics project authority
key_objections_addressed: Fear of errors / debugging: '98% less errors — Bolt automatically tests, refactors, and iterates'
AI tool overload: 'No more AI anxiety or juggling multiple platforms'
Scalability concerns: 'Handle projects 1,000 times larger than before'
Infrastructure complexity: 'Stop stitching together platforms — hosting, databases, auth all built in'
Starting from scratch: 'Stop building from scratch. Start building on-brand.' via design system import
missing_or_weak: No actual customer testimonials or named quotes
The 'trusted by' claim is incomplete — no company names follow it in visible text
No pricing shown on page; tiers and costs are entirely hidden behind a nav link
No live demo, video, or screenshot of the product in action mentioned in text
No guarantees, SLAs, or refund policies
Press mentions entirely absent despite being a well-known product
No specific customer success stories or case studies
Vague on what 'frontier coding agents' means to a non-technical buyer
standout_elements: Design system import is a genuinely differentiated enterprise hook — naming Porsche and Washington Post is credible and aspirational
Role-based value prop section (PM, Entrepreneur, Marketer, etc.) addresses multiple buyer personas without a confusing multi-product feel
Framing '98% less errors' as a headline metric is unusually specific and compelling for this category
'Build now' vs 'Plan' dual CTA neatly segments decisive vs exploratory visitors
Positioning as the convergence point for multiple AI labs ('frontier coding agents') is a smart hedge against model commoditization
